titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Moses Stuart Papers
titlestmt.author: Andover Newton Theological School and Yale Divinity Library staff

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday January 4, 2024 at 23:00

did.unittitle: Moses Stuart Papers
did.abstract: The collection primarily documents Stuart's professional work as a Congregational minister (1806-1810) and professor at Andover Theological Seminary (1810-1848).

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Stuart was born in Wilton, Connecticut on March 26, 1780. He graduated from Yale College in 1799 and was ordained on March 5, 1806 after studying with President Timothy Dwight of Yale. Stuart was pastor of First Church, New Haven, CT from 1806 to 1810. He was Professor of Sacred Literature at Andover Theological Seminary from 1810 to 1848. He died in Andover, MA on January 4, 1852.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The collection was given to Andover Theological Seminary by Sarah Stuart Robbins (daughter) in 1910 and Mary Stuart Anthony (granddaughter) in 1946. It was transferred from Andover Newton Theological School to the Yale Divinity Library in 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The bulk of the collection is arranged according to the order prescribed by Stuart in a manuscript dated April 1837, "Expression of my desire, as to the disposal of my Manuscripts, viz. Lectures, Sermons, Essays, etc., which are not published. [Also in regard to the repubublication of some of my works already published]."

In addition to the items described above, there is some correspondence related to an unsigned review of sermons on the atonement by James Murdock, Daniel Dana and Moses Stuart, which appeared in Christian Spectator , vol. 9, September 1, 1824. Provenance of this material is not known, although the lettets were discovered with the Edwards Amasa Park Papers. Also, there are four letters of the Stuart family, gift of Mary Stuart Anthony.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Moses Stuart Papers (RG 294), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is organized in four series:

did.unittitle: Notes, Lectures, Essays

Note from Stuart: "Expression of my desire, as to the disposal of my Manuscripts, viz. Lectures, Sermons, Essays, etc. which are not published. [Also in regard to the republication of some of my works already published]"
List of sermons
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 1-11

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For full listing of lecture titles, see original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

did.unittitle: Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 12-13
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lecture 14
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lecture 15
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lecture 16
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 17-19
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 20-21
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 22-23
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 24-25
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 26-27
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 28-29
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 30-32
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 33-34
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 35-37
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 38-39
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 40, 42
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 46-47
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 48-49
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lecture 50
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lecture 51
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 52-53
Lectures on Sacred Literature -- Old Testament: Lectures 54-55
Lectures on Hermeneutics: Lectures 1-2
Lectures on Hermeneutics: Lectures 3-4
Lectures on Hermeneutics: Lectures 6-7
Lectures to the Advanced Class; Notes -- Exodus, Psalms, EzekieL, Revelation
Notes on some portions of Ecclesiastes
Notes on the book of Joel, etc.
Letter from James Seixas, with corrections for Prof. Stuart's Hebrew grammar
Hebrew extracts and translations
Notes on Introduction to the Gospels
Notes for an Introduction to the Epistle to the Ephesians
Translation of Tittmann, De Synonymis in Novo Testamento , chapters 1-4
Notes on Sacred Geography
Arabic Grammar
Syriac Grammar
Dissertation on the importance, the difficulties and the practibility of obtaining correct editions of classical books for the use of colleges and schools in the United States
Memoranda Pindarica
Notes on the Phaedo of Plato
Extracts in regard to logos
Extracts from Gerard's Description de l'Egypte
Lecture on slavery (abstract)
Essays on different aspects of the subject of original sin
Address to students
Address to students
Address before the Bible Class Society
Oration on taking my degree
Temperance pledge
Sermons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For full listing of exact dates and scriptural texts of sermons, see original finding, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

did.unittitle: Dated sermons
Dated sermons
Dated sermons
Dated sermons
Dated sermons
Correspondence
Correspondence relating to Christian Spectator review of September 1, 1824, including to Elias Cornelius and from J. Mitchell
Family letters: Moses Stuart to Abigail Clark
Family letters: George Anthony to Abigail Stuart; Austin Phelps, to George Anthony
Microfilm

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This preservation microfilm was prepared by the Andover Newton Theological School library prior to the transfer of the Stuart papers to Yale Divinity Library in 2017. A link to the original finding aid for the collection is available at https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

did.unittitle: Reel Mic 118: Correspondence, lectures, list of sermons 1804-1846, miscellany
Reel Mic 119: Hermeneutics lectures
Reel Mic 120: Lectures, sermons, list of sermons
Reel Mic 121: Notes, sermons
Reel Mic 122: Sermons
